---
title: "About"
toc: true
toc-location: left
lightbox: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
---

```{r setup}
source("_common.R")
```

# Short Bio

Marc Lemus-Canovas is a **Beatriu de Pinós Postdoctoral Fellow** at the [Barcelona Supercomputing Center](https://www.bsc.es/) (Earth Sciences Department, Spain) since February 2026. His research focuses on understanding the link between **atmospheric dynamics** and **extreme meteorological events** — heatwaves, compound droughts, and extreme precipitation — with particular emphasis on the **European Alps** and the **Mediterranean** region. He develops synoptic classification and attribution methods to quantify the role of climate change in shaping these extremes.

He holds a **PhD in Geography** (Extraordinary Doctorate Prize) from the University of Barcelona, and has held postdoctoral positions at EURAC Research (Italy), Andorra Research+Innovation (Andorra), and the University of Santiago de Compostela (Spain). As Principal Investigator, he has secured over **€390,000** in competitive funding, including the **Flash4CastImp** project (Marie Skłodowska-Curie Actions, Horizon Europe, €194,075; 2027–2029), the **HIPEAtMount** project (Province of Bolzano, €172,750), and the **ATOC** project (Barcelona City Council, €24,500).

He is the author of [**synoptReg**](https://lemuscanovas.github.io/synoptreg/), an R package for synoptic climate classification widely used in climatological research. Since May 2025 he coordinates an international network (KNMI, IPSL, SLF, Oxford) to develop an R/Python package for extreme weather attribution using the flow analogues technique. He also participated in the review process of the Sixth IPCC Assessment Report.

# Research Interests

- Synoptic climatology and atmospheric circulation
- Extreme weather events: heatwaves, compound droughts, extreme precipitation
- Climate change attribution in mountain regions (Alps, Pyrenees)
- Compound event analysis
- Urban climatology
- R programming and open-source tools for climate science

# Current Position

**Beatriu de Pinós Postdoctoral Fellow** · *since February 2026*  
Barcelona Supercomputing Center — Earth Sciences Department, Spain

# Previous Positions

| Period | Position | Institution |
|---|---|---|
| Oct 2024 – Feb 2026 | Seal of Excellence Postdoctoral Fellow | EURAC Research, Center for Climate Change and Transformation, Italy |
| Oct 2023 – Sep 2024 | Postdoctoral Researcher (Xunta de Galicia) + Visiting Postdoc | Univ. of Santiago de Compostela / EURAC Research, Italy |
| Oct 2022 – Sep 2023 | Postdoctoral Researcher (Xunta de Galicia) + Visiting Postdoc | Univ. of Santiago de Compostela / Andorra Research+Innovation |
| May 2022 – Aug 2022 | Postdoctoral Researcher | Andorra Research+Innovation |
| Nov 2021 – Feb 2022 | Adjunct Professor | University of Barcelona, Dept. of Geography |
| Oct 2018 – Oct 2021 | Predoctoral Researcher FPU | University of Barcelona, Dept. of Geography |
| Apr 2018 – Sep 2018 | Predoctoral Researcher APIF | University of Barcelona, Dept. of Geography |
| Oct 2017 – Nov 2017 | JAE-Intro Fellow | Spanish National Research Council, Institute of Geosciences |

*Research stays during PhD: University of Lisbon (3 mo), University of Porto (1 mo), University of Santiago de Compostela (1 mo). Scientific secondment: ETH Zurich — Hydrology & Climate Impacts in Mountain Regions Group (Sep–Dec 2025).*

# Education

**PhD in Geography** · University of Barcelona, Spain · 2022 *(Extraordinary Doctorate Prize)*  
**MSc in Remote Sensing and GIS** · Autonomous University of Barcelona, Spain · 2017  
**BSc in Geography** · University of Barcelona, Spain · 2016

# Contact

Feel free to reach out via [email](mailto:lemuscanovas@gmail.com) or through any of the social links at the bottom of this page.

`r last_updated()`
